preamble: Nasal Raising of rounded vowels involves the shift of a nasalized mid vowel [õ] to a high vowel [ũ]. This is part of a common historical raising path where nasalization can drive vowel height changes.

examples.note: A frequent path in the development of nasal vowel systems across various language families.
Raising of nasal vowels in specific unstressed or dialectal environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
